item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Welcome to Purgatory! I gave these folks a chance for revenge and they can't wait to take it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Bit of a leap, but is the first blackbox skip SCP-1304? Given the 'ritual sacrifice' and the death by a family member.

item.title: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. The author may revert at any time.

+ show block
– hide block

Rereading this article for Wikiwalk, I was reminded of SCP-453, in that we have social gatherings on alternating loops. It would not be unreasonable for the SCPF to look into these as potential deviations of the same phenomenon. As such, I am suggesting the following:

Editing this:

All SCP-3248-1 events have been nearly identical, save for the occurrence of an SCP-3248-2 event. At some point during the festivities, one of the partygoers will murder Dr. Sullivan. The method of murder and the partygoer that commits the murder has differed for every SCP-3248-2 event. The partygoers will react with shock, and will attempt to call authorities or, in some cases, attempt to leave. The SCP-3248-1 event will always end at midnight.

To this:

All SCP-3248-1 events have been nearly identical, save for the occurrence of an SCP-3248-2 event. At some point during the festivities, one of the partygoers will murder Dr. Sullivan. The method of murder and the partygoer that commits the murder has differed for every SCP-3248-2 event. The partygoers will react with shock, and will attempt to call authorities or, in some cases, attempt to leave2. The SCP-3248-1 event will always end at midnight1.

Footnotes
1. Similarites between SCP-3248-1 events and the scripts of SCP-453 has been noted. An investigation into whether the two anomalies are derivatives of the same phenomenon is ongoing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gives new meaning to "I'll murder you in the face!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Interesting.

While we do have other time loop skips, I'll give you the credit of being the first one that comes to mind featuring someone eating another's face off.

+1 for some rather disturbing imagery.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This reminds me of the scripted nightclub, in a good way. I wish we had a little more variety to the loops, while the pacing of the log works and the ending is chilling, I think it would be better if you either shortened it or change the ritualistic stuff to something less cliche. I think it works here but the last entry shows there's much more to be mined when the scenario is a little more creative. The bits at the end are good too, but I think if you could introduce a little more variety, maybe expanding on the possible ways to torment Dr. Sullivan? +1 for still being creepy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Murder loops are always fun, and the way it's presented here feels fresh. The difference between SCP-3248-1 events and SCP-3248-2 events isn't explained very clearly, but there's enough to like here that I'm happy to upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
[Spoilers] I think I get it.
This is their revenge, isn't it? He killed them, so they kill him over and over. Forever. And they get crazier with time.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
This is my entry for the 72 hour jam. I don't expect this to work wonders, but here's my shot at a Murder Mystery.

Thanks to Gummy_Dragon and Tanhony for critique.
